overview: This material covers IPv6 only where it intersects other CCNA topics already sourced (ACLs and FHRP) — deep IPv6 addressing/configuration is flagged as covered in an earlier, separate volume.
core_concepts: Cisco devices support IPv6-specific access lists, viewable with 'show access-lists' (the same command used for IPv4 in some contexts).
HSRPv2 supports IPv6; HSRP version 1 does not.
Hosts can learn IPv6 configuration automatically (SLAAC) or via DHCPv6.

verification_commands.purpose: Displays configured access lists, including IPv6 ACLs.

best_practices: Use HSRPv2, not v1, wherever IPv6 default-gateway redundancy is required.
interview_questions: Which HSRP version is required to support IPv6, and why does it matter?
What are the two ways a host can learn its IPv6 configuration?
exam_traps: Assuming HSRP version 1 supports IPv6 — it does not; only HSRPv2 does.
